name: Petitions and Communications
title:
Petitions and Communications received from June 11, 2026, through June 17, 2026, for reference by the President to Committee considering related matters, or to be ordered filed by the Clerk on June 23, 2026.

Personal information that is provided in communications to the Board of Supervisors is subject to disclosure under the California Public Records Act and the San Francisco Sunshine Ordinance. Personal information will not be redacted.

From the Office of the Mayor (MYR), pursuant to Charter, Section 3.100, submitting an Acting Mayor Notice designating Supervisor Matt Dorsey as Acting-Mayor effective Thursday, June 11, 2026 at 7:00 PM until Friday, June 12, 2026 at 11:59 PM; Supervisor Bilaal Mahmood as Acting-Mayor effective Saturday, June 13, 2026 at 12:00 AM until 11:59 PM; Supervisor Connie Chan as Acting-Mayor effective Sunday, June 14, 2026 at 12:00 AM until 11:59 PM; and Board President Mandelman as Acting-Mayor effective Monday, June 15, 2026 at 12:00 AM until 11:59 PM . Copy: Each Supervisor. (1)

From the Department of Elections (REG), submitting the certification for the initiative measure entitled “Parcel Tax to Fund Public Transportation.” Copy: Each Supervisor. (2)

From the Office of the Treasurer and Tax Collector (TTX), pursuant to California State Government Code, Section 53646, submitting the Monthly Pooled Investment Report for the month of May 2026. Copy: Each Supervisor. (3)

From the San Francisco Police Department (SFPD), pursuant to Administrative Code, Chapter 96A, submitting First Quarter (Q1) of Calendar Year (CY) 2026 Quarterly Activity and Data Report (QADR). Copy: Each Supervisor. (4)

From the Sheriff’s Office (SHF), regarding the Resolution authorizing the Sheriff’s Office to contract with the San Francisco Pretrial Diversion Project (SF Pretrial) for Pretrial Services for a three year period from July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2029, for a contract total not to exceed amount of $22,532,145, with two one-year options to extend. File No. 260442. Resolution No. 334-26. Copy: Each Supervisor. (5)

From members of the public, regarding District 9. 2 Letters. Copy: Each Supervisor. (6)

From members of the public, regarding the San Francisco Budget. File Nos. 260616, 260596, and 260597. 8 Letters. Copy: Each Supervisor. (7)

From members of the public, regarding the renovation of the Crocker Amazon baseball and softball fields. 8 Letters. Copy: Each Supervisor. (8)

From members of the public, regarding the San Francisco Giants. 10 Letters. Copy: Each Supervisor. (9)

From members of the public, regarding banning retail animal sales. 17 Letters. Copy: Each Supervisor. (10)

From Barklee Sanders, regarding the Health Care Security Ordinance (HCSO). Copy: Each Supervisor. (11)

From Katherine Mendonca, regarding taxes on small businesses. Copy: Each Supervisor. (12)

From John Bove, regarding Capp Street and Shotwell Street. Copy: Each Supervisor. (13)

From George Soler, regarding emergency vehicles. Copy: Each Supervisor. (14)

From Holly Gustafson, regarding illegal refuse dumping. Copy: Each Supervisor. (15)

From Beth Muzzy, regarding the North of Panhandle and Western Addition Districts. Copy: Each Supervisor. (16)

From Bill Lo, regarding the San Francisco Public Library. Copy: Each Supervisor. (17)

From Thomas Luchini, regarding Resolution urging the San Francisco Municipal Transportation Agency to implement red zones for daylighting at all crosswalks and intersections to comply with the Daylighting to Save Lives Bill.  File No. 241180. Resolution No. 632-24. Copy: Each Supervisor. (18)

From Bruce Sanchez, regarding parking. Copy: Each Supervisor. (19)

From Todd Greenberg, regarding Municipal Elections Code - Ballot Argument Fees and Municipal Elections Code - Voter Information Pamphlets. File Nos. 260603 and 260604. Copy: Each Supervisor. (20)

From members of the public, regarding banning plastic cigarette filters. 21 Letters. Copy: Each Supervisor. (21)

From members of the public, regarding Planning Code - Transit-Oriented
Residential Development. File No. 260132 Ordinance No. 82-26. 2 Letters. Copy: Each Supervisor. (22)

From members of the public, regarding the Resolution authorizing the Sheriff’s Office to contract with the San Francisco Pretrial Diversion Project (SF Pretrial) for Pretrial Services for a three year period from July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2029, for a contract total not to exceed amount of $22,532,145, with two one-year options to extend. File No. 260442. Resolution No. 334-26. 31 Letters. Copy: Each Supervisor. (23)

From members of the public, regarding the Hearing of the Board of Supervisors sitting as a Committee of the Whole on June 16, 2026, at 3:00 p.m., to consider the Department of Public Health's budget reducing medical and health care services provided by the City and County of San Francisco for Fiscal Years (FYs) 2026-2027 and 2027-2028, pursuant to California Health and Safety Code, Section 1442.5(a) (The Beilenson Act). File No. 260565. 5 Letters. Copy: Each Supervisor. (24)

From a member of the public, regarding the proposed Ordinance amending the Health Code to prohibit smoking in outdoor patios of bars and taverns; eliminate exceptions allowing indoor smoking in bars with no employees, bars with historically compliant semi-enclosed smoking rooms, and hotel rooms, to conform to provisions of California law; and repeal suspended and superseded provisions regulating smoking in certain locations. File No. 260361. Copy: Each Supervisor. (25)

From the Planning Department (CPC), submitting an Interim Zoning Controls - Conditional Use Authorization for Outdoor Laboratory Uses in PDR-1-G post-passage report for File No. 251116. Copy: Each Supervisor. (26)
